name: 101 Outdoor Arts
description: 101 supports artists from all over the world who make work for the outdoors and public space, through a year round programme of artistic residencies, professional development events, and a dedicated design and fabrication workshop. Artists from a wide range of disciplines can make use of our large and intimate rehearsal spaces, staying onsite in our artists’ village. As part of Corn Exchange Newbury we present vibrant and wide-reaching outdoor events in partnership with our local communities, bringing thousands of people together each year to experience a free international programme of events
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Support Organisation

name: 18Hours
description: 18 Hours delivers events and education with a focus on global citizenship and diversity Clients include The British Council, Hastings Borough Council, Rother District Council, University of Brighton, Hastings Storytelling Festival, Big Local, Streets of Rother Festival, Big Local and St Leonards Festival.We also produce Journeys Dance Festival, our own annual event.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Educational Organisation, Producer / Programmer / Administrator, Production Manager / Technician / Maker

name: 2Faced Dance Company
description: 2Faced Dance Company produces innovative, accomplished and aspirational artistic performance and participation programmes alongside a captivating community dance programme that truly reflects the landscape in which we are based, allowing us to tell new and original stories, generating distinctive new work, whilst striving to stay relevant to our audiences. The company tours extensively across the UK and internationally aiming to work with cutting edge and culturally diverse designers, venues, organisations and people. We aim to support and mentor local artists whilst advocating on behalf of th

name: 6FootStories
description: We are6FootStories, proud purveyors of interactive silliness and theatrical roguery. We make a mixture of original devised work, reworked Shakespeare and anarchic interactive content.  We began as a collaboration between writer-performers Jake Hassam and Nigel Munson, who met whilst training at the Oxford School of Drama, but have grown into a core collective of performers, makers and creatives, with the power of the ensemble becoming an essential part of our creative process in recent years. We have been proudly supported by Arts Council England on a number of projects, including our sci-fi c
member_type: Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative, Producer / Programmer / Administrator

name: Activate Performing Arts
description: For over 30 years, we’ve been promoting, supporting and producing some of the most exciting events in the UK. We’re here to bring performing arts to as many people as possible. And give creatives the support they need to make outstanding work. We bring world-class events to unexpected places, like town centres, village squares, beaches and hilltops. And we’ve been doing it for over 30 years.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Festival, Producer / Programmer / Administrator, Production Manager / Technician / Maker, Support Organisation

name: Adam Towndrow
description: Creative Producer, Adam Towndrow, has extensive experience in the outdoor and indoor sector working alongside organisations to create high quality works, strategising company objectives and supporting/challenging artists experiences within the industry. Co-Founded C-12 Dance Theatre, Adam Produced award winning and international touring productions including Trolleys (Choreographed by Shaun Parker), Market Stall, The Van Man and Secret Encounters (2005 – 2022).  Supported the development of IJAD Dance Company (2018 – 2019), Produced BEAUTiFUL by Sally Marie (UK Tour) (2018).

name: Adrenalism
description: Adrenalism is a theatre companybased in Glasgow, Scotland but make work for everywhere. Our projects are entertaining, accessible and connected to the world we all live in.

name: Adventure Arts
description: “Hello! My name is Adam and I’m the artistic director of Adventure Arts. I’ve worked in theatre and CommunityArts for over 15 years (or have I been saying that for a few years and need to update?). My background is in physical and visual theatre including modern clowning, improvisation, storytelling, puppetry, mask and movement.” Adventure Arts is a performance and arts organisation based in Todmorden, West Yorkshire. We look to bring amazing performances, workshops and above all fun to as many people as we can. We work in lots of different mediums; story-telling, puppetry, games, crafts- the

name: Aimee Williamson
description: Aimee is a Lancashire based performer, dance artist and theatre maker. Her work spans across ballet, contemporary, physical theatre and puppetry. She creates work for outdoor spaces using inspiration from folklore, nature and local heritage. Her work includes traditional folk dancing of Morris and Lancashire clog and modern styles of dance. She feels a strong connection to her home area and strives to bring high quality dance theatre and the warmth of folk storytelling to local family audiences.

name: Alexandra Tsiapi
description: Alexandra Tsiapi is a freelance Dance and Movement Artist specialized in performance and choreography. She is part of “Vaiven Movement Collective” where she explores her practice through film, outdoor work and site specific work. Alexandra has performed and choreographed work for outdoor festivals, theatre spaces, unconventional spaces, studios. Alexandra works with movement improvisation, characterization and voice in order to bring to life lively stories and narratives. Through her work, Alexandra looks at dance and ecology aiming to promote environmental awareness, and change the way we see

name: Alistair Aitcheson
description: Alistair has been a professional video game developer since 2010, and brings his design and tech skills to the stage in playable stage shows. His debut showThe Incredible Playable Show, created in 2016, uses unusual custom-made video game controllers to get the audience up on stage, moving around and making each other laugh. Controllers include inflatables, tablets worn on the body, and barcode scanners. It won the Jury Choice Award at the IndieCade Festival in Los Angeles in 2017

name: All or Nothing Aerial Dance Theatre
description: All or Nothing are Scotland’s leading aerial dance company creating, performing, teaching and pioneering aerial arts in Scotland. Our work ranges from small scale theatre to large scale outdoor spectacle. Hanging from boats, buildings and cranes, flying through forests, across lochs or dancing on iconic landmarks. All or Nothing combine aerial arts with dance theatre and contemporary circus, creating their own theatrical works for touring as well as developing aerial elements for events. Participation is vital to the company’s work, believing that aerial is for everyone, developing all kinds o
member_type: Educational Organisation, Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative

name: Altered Scale Theatre
description: Altered Scale Theatre is a storytelling, puppetry and theatre company that focuses on creating exceptional performances for all ages.​ We also collaborate with other companies making puppets for theatre, outdoor events as well as commissions.

name: Altered Skin
description: Formed in 2013 by Creative Director Shane Shambhu, Altered Skin is an international multi-lingual physical theatre company that shares transcultural narratives through merging artforms and cultures from global perspectives. As a pioneering company leading the way for a fresh, new theatrical exploration of the South Indian dance language of bharatanatyam. All our work is original and created through collaboration using a culturally different creative thinking leading to our unique approach to performance. Our distinct brand of humour and Indian dance inspired theatre taps into the imagination i

name: Amadan Ensemble
description: Premier theatre company on the island of Ireland specialising in the artistry and mastery of clown and buffoon as a tool to entertain, enlighten and challenge audiences Amadan Ensemble makes devised theatre specialising in the practice of Clown & Bouffon. We create and produce original work that takes risks, touring productions throughout ROI and NI since 2014. Our work is accessible, stimulating, relevant, entertaining and challenging for audiences, encouraging interaction and engagement.
region: Northern Ireland Premier Company Specialising in the Styles of Clown and Bouffon

name: An Indian Summer Festival 2026
description: Inspirate’s flagship festival, An Indian Summer, will be back next year, bringing a new and unique programme of contemporary South Asian arts and culture to Leicester! Celebrating the shared culture between South Asia and the UK – we are bringing communities together through bold artistic collaborations with a focus on creating work within public and outdoor spaces. Celebrating the shared culture between South Asia and the UK – we are bringing communities together through bold artistic collaborations with a focus on creating work within public and outdoor spaces.

name: Angie Mack
description: Based in the UK this highly skilled, creative and captivating hula hooper has been wowing audiences for well over a decade, performing and teaching at a wide array of different events across the globe. She has featured in music videos, worked in television, private parties, team building events as well as projects with community groups, weddings, corporate parties, theatres, festivals and more.

name: Angus MacKechnie
description: Angus is a programmer, creative producer and outdoor arts, circus and theatre specialist. He is Creative Director and Joint CEO of Jacksons Lane. He was previously Director ofOutdoorArtsUK for eight years,Creative Director and CEO of Imagineer Productions in Coventryand prior to that,he worked at the National Theatre in London for over twenty years, serving underfive different directorships and running a wide variety of projects, largely related to audience engagement. He became Producer and Programmer of two National Theatre departments:theWatch This Space Festival of national and internation

name: Animate Puppet Co
description: Animate is an emerging puppetry theatre company, based in Winchester. We create striking and imaginative theatre experiences, run engaging puppetry workshops, and inclusive community events.

name: Anjali Dance Company
description: Anjali Dance Company celebrates the creative abilities and artistic potential of people with learning disabilities. Through its innovative and pioneering work, Anjali demonstrates exciting new possibilities in dance and engages the professional dance community in a debate about aesthetics, form, purpose and inclusion.
region: England, London, England, Midlands, England, North, England, South East, England, South West

name: Applause
description: We are a cultural organisation, funded by Arts Council England. We are here to develop opportunities for rural communities to work with us, and to provide engaging cultural experiences for all. Applause is cultural charitable organisation working with people across Kent, Sussex, Essex and the wider region to create opportunities for creative experience close to home.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Support Organisation

name: Apus Productions Ltd
description: Apus Productions are an established street theatre production company and have been touring walkabout street theatre since 2007.They are based in Hull which was named Uk City of Culture in 2017. ​They have a current repetoire of eight street theatre acts that tour nationally and internationally. All the acts are walkabout mix and mingle with two having static performable show options. They are all family friendly and can be performed during the day or night time illuminated.

name: Architects of Air Luminarium by Alan Parkinson
description: A Luminarium is designed to bring about an encounter with the phenomenon of light. An encounter that is beautiful in its simplicity which can nevertheless be profound in its effect. Once inside, visitors are immersed into a world of shifting subtle and saturated hues where they may wander, sit down, and lose themselves in sensory bliss – awakening the senses. A paradox, simultaneously providing a stimulating and calming environment, compelling many to rest, meander, or meditate.

name: Art In The Park
description: Royal Leamington Spa’s Art in the Park Festival is a free curated arts festival with a difference. We showcase, sell and provide live demonstrations from artists and craftspeople in Warwickshire and the Midlands. We celebrate independent local and regional food and drink. We programme the best live music, dance and theatre from our own doorstep alongside nationally and internationally renowned companies. We do it over 2 days in August in the beautiful surroundings of Jephson Gardens and Mill Gardens. ​Art in the Park is a Community Interest Company. We rely on the generosity of our sponsors, t

name: Art Reach
description: Art Reach ignites imaginations, inspires people and promotes greater understanding of the world around us. We are conversation starters and thought provokers. We’re bold in our approach, committed to delivering art with energy, authenticity and ambition.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Festival, Support Organisation
region: England, London, England, Midlands, England, North, England, South East, England, South West, Europe

name: Artemis Productions UK
description: Artemis Productions was established in 2000. Based in Liverpool, we are a street theatre company that specialises in vibrant stilt walking characters and interactive performance. We have built on our reputation of producing high-quality stilt characters and regularly perform nationally and internationally at festivals, carnivals, corporate and council events. The core company members are Barry Han, a musician, designer, costume maker and Sarah Han an actress, costume maker and workshop facilitator. Artemis thrive on being challenged to create new characters based on a brief and some of our mos

name: Articulation
description: Articulation is an advocacy and development organisation for the circus, outdoor arts and physical performance sector in Scotland. Articulation works strategically, in partnership with artists and organisations across the sector, to champion home grown talent on a local and international level, to give agency to under-represented artists, and to promote and develop high quality work. Articulation is committed to supporting a thriving and robust sector, and to encouraging diversity, innovation and sustainability.

name: Articulture Wales
description: From radical theatre and world-class circus to international sell-out light and sound shows and Europe’s largest touring festival, outdoor arts in Wales can be found on your street, in your marketplace, city centre, national park, mountains and beaches. Outdoor Arts in Wales encompasses a wealth of Welsh talent from theatre, traditional and contemporary dance, music and puppetry, to comedy, pyrotechnics, circus, and visual and digital art.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Support Organisation

name: Artizani
description: Artizani started in 1989 with an old Ford Escort van and a vision of making performances on the streets. 30+ years later we are still doing it, using all the lessons we learnt on the way (although the van died in 1994). Since then we have won awards, been commissioned multiple times, secured ACE funding for several projects and toured all over the world and to most of the UK’s towns and cities. We are experts in creating outdoor arts, time-served specialists in the form – we don’t just put art on the streets, we make art for the streets. Public space can be a challenging environment in which t

name: Ascension Dance Company
description: Created in 2017 by Ashley Jordan & Ben Morley, they are committed to creating high quality movement provisions for young people and eye-catching performance work with human connection and raw physicality at the centre. Ascension Danceare now developing their careers and expertise in dance, movement and set work with the aim to create opportunities for the next generation of movement artists in the West Midlands.

name: Aswarm
description: Working with Places, their Communities, utilising Sound, Sculpture, XR/MR/AR, Performance and Interactive Technologies Aswarm works in collaboration with a small team of creative professionals and a wider network of artists, makers, programmers, super-geeks, architects, designers and performers.

name: Autin Dance Theatre
description: Autin Dance Theatre is an innovative and interdisciplinary touring dance theatre company based in Birmingham (UK), established since 2013 under the helm of director/choreographer Johnny Autin. Autin Dance Theatre is a collaborative and project-based arts organisation that produces engaging artistic performances and provides extensive learning & participation programmes in the community. AutinDT’s mission is to inspire, educate and instigate positive social changes by delivering world-class multi-disciplinary arts activities. Since 2013, we have built a strong reputation for our work internatio

name: Avanti Promotions Entertainment Agency
description: July 1982 and the BBC presented the final show at Arena North, Park Hall, Wigan when I purchased a number of games and props from the production team and hit the road with the first touring version of It’s a Knockout, ending with two units on the road as it proved so popular. Finally selling the show to Graham Fisher and moving on to a new venture. ‘The Crazy Bears’ show came to life with its first performance at Golden Square Shopping Centre and ran for nineteen years taking the mobile stage show to all corners of the UK, Ireland and even Kuwait.
region: England, Midlands, England, North, England, South East, England, South West

name: B arts
description: B arts started life as three women and a bike making character-based, walkabout, comedy street theatre. They soon realised that this was no life for respectable women with bills to pay and possibly wouldn’t get the patriarchy dismantled anytime soon. After encountering and working with Welfare State International everything changed. They started making collaborative theatre, carnivals, lantern processions, collaborative creative projects of all sorts, digging into communities and mining for meaning. B arts built partnerships in Italy, Hungary, Rumania, Sweden, Bosnia and France, making work th
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Performance Company

name: Babbling Vagabonds
description: We are a theatre arts company based in Buxton.We have over 20 years of experience in creating work for theatres, festivals, schools, and communities.  Our aim is to create heartwarming theatrical adventures for people of all ages. Sharing the journey with audiences, artists, and partners on a path that is daring, delightful, and inspirational.A good story is at the heart of all our work.This means that we devise all our productions and projects. We approach each project as artists and scientists; with open minds. Searching for the sparks of excitement and inspiration to guide our path. We set

name: BAFA
description: The British Arts Festivals Association (BAFA) was founded in 1969 and is the national development agency for arts festivals, representing some 80 members across the UK. BAFA works with its members to raise the profile of festivals, lobby on issues relevant to the sector and to offer tailored support, networking and development for festival organisers, enabling its members to develop extraordinary events in a stable, creative and supportive environment. BAFA connects arts festivals together in order to create a support network for each other; BAFA members enjoy access to a wide network of their
member_type: British Arts Festivals Association (BAFA) is the Leading Network and Development Agency for UK Arts Festivals
region: The British Arts Festivals Association (BAFA) was founded in 1969 and is the national development agency for arts festivals, representing some 80 members across the UK. BAFA works with its members to raise the profile of festivals, lobby on issues relevant to the sector and to offer tailored support, networking and development for festival organisers, enabling its members to develop extraordinary events in a stable, creative and supportive environment.

name: Barmpot Theatre
description: BARMPOT THEATRE is based in the heart of the Lincolnshire Wolds, and create and deliver puppet theatre performances for children and families. Combining our trademark style of physical performance, puppetry and original music, we specialise in taking top quality theatre to rural communities, and telling untold Lincolnshire stories to a wider audience across the UK.

name: Bash Street Theatre
description: Bash Street is a small independent theatre company founded in 1991 by partners, Simon Pullum and JoJo Pickering. The show is designed for both indoor and outdoor touring, with a special 40-minute version tailored for street festivals, and is suitable for all ages.

name: Basingstoke Festival
description: Sign up to our newsletter,our advocacy piece for Outdoor Arts, where you’ll receive news, sector information, and updates about upcoming events, performances and festivals straight to your inbox. Join OAUK to receive member benefitse.g. members newsletter, blog, support, resources & opportunities.

name: Bell and Bullock Street Theatre
description: Together they founded their company in 1991 and 30 years later are still going strong. This street theatre duo bring you gentle comedy and creative nonsense with their highly participatory shows and walkabout acts. Touring (inter) nationally, they have built a reputation for entertaining the crowds at events and festivals all year round. They met in their home town of Bristol whilst attending training at Fool Time and discovered a shared passion for circus, clowning and street theatre. Karen went on to do the Drama degree at Newcastle Poly and lured Dafe up north to join her. Once there, his b

name: Beside Ourselves
description: Beside Ourselves create bold, dynamic and generous interactive performance for adult and family audiences. Our playful curiosity creates meaningful connection with participants and our home-grown aesthetic and upcycled costumes make us approachable as we engage with local culture and communities. We are mentored by Wet Picnic and directed by international clown performer-director Deanna Fleysher (Butt Kapinsky.)

name: Bettina Linstrum
description: Having started as Creative Producer with my company ArtsAgenda, I now focus on my Therapeutic Coaching practice and Leadership Development for the cultural industry. As a freelancer, I know how tough it can be to stay on your track, and focus your energy on what brings you and your career forward and make sustainable decisions that meet your needs. Alongside Therapeutic Coaching techniques, I am also trained in Clean Language Coaching, which uses metaphor, drawing and space. It’s elegant, it’s creative, it is surprising, it can create profound shifts.“Coaching has been the greatest investment

name: Bev Adams Arts
description: I create excellent outdoor arts productions, inclusive festivals and community events with an emphasis on social purpose, diversity and community participation. My projects engage people from all walks of life, helping them to connect with each other and to explore the worlds in which they live.
member_type: Performer / Artist / Creative, Producer / Programmer / Administrator

name: Bigbubbleman
description: For over ten years I have been a familiar sight at outdoor events, especially in Derbyshire, Cheshire, South Yorkshire, Greater Manchester and Staffordshire but also further afield. If it is not too windy, I make bubbles so enormous that people will gasp in amazement.  I continually switch between giant bubbles, and vast torrents of smaller bubbles, with all conceivable types of bubbles in between. Meanwhile, I release a continuous ‘bubble blizzard’ with battery-powered bubble machine and my wonderful wind-powered Bubble Mill invention. I also provide equipment for children to create their own

name: Birdcage Stage
description: The Birdcage Stage is a touring outdoor arts organisation specialising in creating visual theatre using a giant golden birdcage structure. They are a collective of artists, circus performers and musicians with a shared vision of making magical and joyful theatrical experiences for all, no matter age, race or background. They use puppetry, aerial theatre, clowning and music to create memorable events that are heart- warming, thought provoking and inspiring, celebrating themes of nature, folklore, mythology, female empowerment, and the environment. The Birdcage has toured to festivals as a uniqu
member_type: Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative, Production Manager / Technician / Maker, Venue

name: Birmingham Hippodrome Festivals
description: We are on a mission to enrich the cultural life of the region. Our vision is clear; we are creating a world-class cultural scene for our region with Birmingham Hippodrome firmly centre stage. As the cultural beating heart of the city, Birmingham Hippodrome reaches over one million people a year through on and off-stage programmes of work, digital presence, and performances in city and beyond. Birmingham Hippodrome is an independent charity, run by a board of voluntary trustees with no annual revenue funding from public sources.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Festival, Venue

name: Bloomsbury Festival
description: Bloomsbury Festival is an annual celebration of the area’s creativity, presenting an inspiring programme of culture, arts, science, literature, performance, discussion and debate. The programme includes an exciting mix of contemporary arts, music, performance, theatre, science displays, talks, walks and community events. Established in 2006, Bloomsbury Festival is a creative explosion of arts, culture and science.  For ten days each October, the streets, parks, museums, galleries, laboratories and public and private buildings of this vibrant cultural quarter play host up to 130 events which at

name: Bolton Gate Arts
description: Once the well loved and remembered saddlery stables, Bolton Gate Farm now operates as a family-run artisan cafe, and rural outdoor arts venue with a variety of events throughout the year including live theatre, music, markets, craft activities and much more. Located on the outskirts of Weston Coyney, and with a stunning natural backdrop, we have created a space that serves its communities, sources locally and works with the people of Stoke-on-Trent and the Staffordshire Moorlands, as a space for relaxation and creativity.

name: Bradford 2025
description: Bradford has been revealed as the winner of the hotly contested UK City of Culture 2025 competition. The year is set to deliver more than 1000 new performances and events including 365 artist commissions, a series of major arts festivals as well as exciting national and international collaborations.

name: BrainFools
description: Alright, folks! Brace yourselves for a mind-blowing, never-seen-before dose of circus madness with BrainFools! Get ready to have your reality flipped, your laughter unleashed, and your imagination set free? We’re Finn and Toffy, the mischievous duo behind this circus revolution, and we’re here to rock your world, my friend! We’re fresh outta circus school, bursting with creativity, and ready to blow your mind with our mind-bending shows. Trust us, we know how to put on a performance that’ll leave you gobsmacked!

name: Brave Bold Drama
description: People deserve high quality arts in their lives, even if they can’t pay. Brave Bold Drama is led by two freelance artists who are working class and LGBTQI+. We make award-winning theatre for families and for those living with dementia, and run a broad portfolio of community art projects made with and for working class communities.
member_type: Educational Organisation, Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative

name: Brighton Open Air Theatre
description: In April 2013 Brighton playwright and construction manager, Adrian Bunting, was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer. He died in May 2013, aged 47. In his final days Adrian threw his efforts into realising a long held ambition; the creation of a permanent open-air theatre for the city he loved. Adrian identified the perfect site, the redundant bowling green in Dyke Road Park, drew up designs and left his life savings of £18,000 to kick-start the project. Shortly before he passed away he asked four friends – Steve Turner, Claire Rafferty, James Payne and Donna Close – if they would see his dream th

name: BT Events
description: BT Events provide a complete production solution to a wide range of events, festivals and celebrations. From managing and facilitating bespoke corporate events to multi-day festivals, we offer a tailored solution to meet your needs. Working alongside industry specialists, we draw on professional knowledge and infrastructure to best meet the demands of each unique event, ensuring a unique solution for each client.
member_type: Production Manager / Technician / Maker, Safety / Security

name: Bumble Movement Arts
description: Bumble Movement Arts is a contemporary circus organisation inspired by bees and their dance communication. We too share critical information through movement arts. Artistic director Poppy Emer Greenford is a circus performer and director with a BA and MA in the artform, accredited through Circomedia and Bath Spa University. Her continued research focuses on circus and theatre integration methods. Finding ways to create enjoyable and meaningful performance work around human’s complex relationship with nature.

name: Bureau Of Silly Ideas
description: The Bureau Of Silly Ideas (BOSI) has been putting inspired madness and controlled chaos into public spaces for nearly 20 years. Using art, entertainment, humour and surprise we’ve honed our unique ability to transform the everyday and create magical interventions that bring silliness to the mundane. We’ve got a complete fleet of mad-hat machines and eccentric interactions, most loved are ourStreet Furniture Menagerie, closely followed by ourPedal Powered Pineapple CarandMichael Foot(who regularly shows up onHave I Got News For You).Shirt or Squirt andSecret Squirtersuse gameplay and water to m

name: Cabasa CIC
description: Cabasa is a carnival arts organisation which brings all people together to create big, bold and beautiful outdoor events that capture the joy of being part of a community. Cabasa uses arts and creativity to enable people to learn new skills, develop high-quality work they feel proud of, bring communities together and explore diverse community traditions. We want to see a world in which everyone has access to creative opportunities through Carnival.
member_type: Educational Organisation, Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative

name: Calico
description: Calicois a female-led arts and activism social enterprise, using theatre, film and virtual reality to combat gendered violence. Calico was founded by Daz Scott and Martha Harrison, in direct response to the murder of Sarah Everard. This event occurred during our final year at the University of Birmingham. In response to this tragic event, victim-blaming narratives were prevalent, with media responses suggesting that Sarah should’ve ‘flagged down a bus’ or been more ‘street wise’ to avoid being attacked. We, and our community, were outraged by these narratives and wanted to do something about i

name: Cambridge City Council
description: Cambridge City Council’s Cultural Services deliver a vibrant and diverse programme of events for the whole community making Cambridge a great place to live, work, study and visit. City Events, organise a range of free outdoor events from our newest edition, established in 2023, Out of the Ordinary Festival showcases a free weekend of outdoor arts in the city centre each summer. Our much-loved annual free non-ticketed Fireworks Night attracting over 20,000 attendees. Plus, our Music in the Parks, a free summer series of Sunday afternoon concerts in Cambridge’s beautiful green spaces across the

name: Caoimhe Dunn
description: I’m a Wexford based artist with 17 years experience working in the arts. An IADT graduate of both Fine Art and Model Making, I have been involved for many years in Street Arts & Spectacle, Community and Youth Arts. I’m passionate about community involvement in Street Arts & Spectacle and many of my projects have been community led – teaching skills and devising work with community groups. My work relies on the participation of groups to bring ideas to life. Passing my skills on to others has always played a large part in my own practice. Working as an artist and having the opportunity to demon

name: Carole Keating
description: Carole Keating is an Irish Mancunian artist and producer. She works across disciplines including live art, immersive theatre and interactive play, following the artist-as-initiator model. Her work responds to the injustices and inequalities of late capitalist society, crafting collaborative and empowering spaces of live creative activity co-created with audiences. Her practice is fiercely inclusive and is rooted in joy, care, tactility and play. Influences include the mischievous playfulness and straight-talking, anti-elitist inclusivity of Jeremy Deller, the transformational ambitions of Tani

name: Cassie Catchpole
description: Cassie Catchpole has worked in street performance for 10 years traveling all over the world with The Grand Theatre of Lemmings. She walks on stilts, dresses up as King Kong and tours a tiny caravan theatre around England!

name: Casson & Friends
description: Under the direction of Tim Casson, Casson & Friends are a UK based Record Breaking Dance Theatre Company, creating unique performances that are accessible, interactive and joyful. The company works with a diverse range of collaborators, from filmmakers and musicians to computer programmers and psychologists, but most often collaborates with the public and communities, inviting them to engage with dance in exciting and accessible ways. With a passion for bringing dance to people in new and unusual contexts, C&F’s work to date has been presented in a range of settings including Shopping Centres,
region: Record Breaking Dance Theatre Company creating #PeoplePoweredPerformance since 2012

name: Cecil Green Arts
description: Cecil Green Arts is an artist-led organisation based in Bradford that works with communities to create street theatre and events, specialising in large scale lanterns and puppets. Cecil Green Arts was conceived out of a creative fire-bolt of inspiration in 2011 and the artistic team have enjoyed working together ever since.

name: Charles Brockbank
description: I am a juggler and clown who has spent the last 18 years working on my technical discipline, using traditional juggling props and exploring unusual object manipulation. My current work explores both technical and spectacular juggling with a particular interest in presence, creating live authentic experiences. My performance work has taken me to international arts festivals including CIRCa Circus Festival Auch, Edinburgh Fringe and Stockton International Riverside Festival. As well as devising my own work I have collaborated with a number of established companies, including Desperate Men, The R
member_type: Performer / Artist / Creative, Producer / Programmer / Administrator

name: Chelsey Everatt – EverArts
description: Chelsey Everatt (EverArts) is an experienced Cultural Projects Producer who has worked in the sector for over a decade, independently, in arts organisations and local authorities in the East Midlands, Yorkshire and Humber, and beyond. Chelsey is based in Lincoln, UK. With a degree in Architecture, and Masters in Arts and Cultural Enterprise, her varied background has included: working on the first Lincoln Frequency Festival of Digital Culture; the Paul Hamlyn funded Young People’s Programme with Lincolnshire One Venues; leading community engagement for the Heritage Lottery funded Boultham Park

name: Cheshire Dance
description: Cheshire Dance creates opportunities for people of all ages, abilities and backgrounds to access dance and to use the art form to live healthier and happier lives. From delivering dance classes in the community, to providing professional development & training to emerging artists and hosting events and festivals, we seek to remove inequalities and co-create opportunity to experience the power of dance. Founded as a charity in 1976, over the past four decades Cheshire Dance has enabled hundreds of thousands of individuals to engage with dance. We have enticed a wide variety of people from all w

name: Cimera Circus and Arts – Syrcas a chelfyddydau
description: Cimera are a contemporary circus, theatre and arts company founded in 2013. Like our namesake the dazzling hybrid of Greek Mythology we are a company of many parts. We bring together artists from diverse performance and design perspectives to create a powerful artistic voice for north Wales. We produce original works of street theatre, walkabout stilt characters, touring circus theatre productions, fire shows, arts engagement and education projects, community parades and lantern events, puppet shows, circus and interdisciplinary arts training and workshops for children and adults.

name: Circo Rum Ba Ba
description: Circo Rum Ba Ba are the doyens of interactive circus theatre. We fuse a distinctive mix of circus, physical theatre and elaborate spectacle to craft a surprising exhibition of street theatre where you least expect to find it. For over 20 years we have presented new shows to bring inventive performance at free-to-the-public events all over the UK, Europe and worldwide.

name: Circus Hub
description: Circus Hub is a Community Interest Company limited by guarantee (Registered in England and Wales, No: 14385263), trading as Circus Hub CIC. The owners of Circus Hub are Kay Monrose and Ria Hobbs. Circus Hub’s vision is to transform lives through circus arts, we believe that everyone should be able to have the opportunity to experience circus arts if they wish to.

name: Circus Pazaz  – The Little Circus with Big Fun!
city: family friendly big top circus shows and workshops for events since 2013.
description: Circus Pazaz was founded in 2013 and has been providing high quality, family friendly entertainment to events across the UK ever since. We pride ourselves on consistently delivering circus shows and workshops that engage audiences of all ages, and leave them with fun filled memories for years to come. Whether it’s having learnt to juggle or spin a plate with one of our passionate and experienced circus tutors, or being part of the audience watching one of our exciting circus shows, you will come away from your experience with Circus Pazaz with a smile on your face. We have a growing team of fa
region: Providing high quality, family friendly big top circus shows and workshops for events since 2013.

name: Circus250
description: Circus250 is dedicated to bringing circus to new audiences in unusual places. We believe circus is the most accessible of art forms, for everyone. Even in these challenging times, we work with you to bring invention and joy.Productions include: Circus250 grew out of the UK and Ireland wide celebrations of250 years of circus in 2018.We can only invent a new future if we know what’s come before.
region: England, London, England, Midlands, England, North, England, South East, England, South West, Ireland, Scotland, Wales

name: Cirque Bijou
description: Billy and Julian (co-Artistic Directors) are circus artists, entrepreneurs and creative directors who met in the 90s in Bristol in a performance collective specialising in fire and pyrotechnic shows. They could see a gap in the market for an accessible and popular form of modern circus, and formed Cirque Bijou in 1999. They brought in key creatives and brilliant local and national circus artists to help them make shows that told stories and connected with people, whoever the audience was.

name: Citrus Arts
description: For over 10 years we have been making highly physical and visual performances that combines circus, theatre and dance, which are performed with our communities and also at events, venues and festivals across the UK. Our home is Pontypridd, in the Rhondda Welsh Valleys, 10 minutes from Cardiff and a lot of our activity happens at our community space, Hopkinstown Hall. Bridie and James’ training was in physical theatre and circus. We toured for many years and our expertise is about bringing people together through creativity. We are a not-for-profit charity, originally a family run company but n

name: City Arts Nottingham
description: We break down barriers to reconnect people with art, culture and creativity. Every kind of barrier: economic, geographical, societal, emotional. Because creativity is a human right. Since 1977, we’ve collaborated with communities and artists to create and explore art of all kinds. We make sure our work is inclusive and relevant by listening to what communities need.

name: ClemLive
description: Jackie Clementines is a performance artist, workshop facilitator, fire choreographer, youth worker, writer, activist and clown. In his time off he is just a clown. He has toured original circus, theatre and music across numerous countries, receiving some exceptional reviews along the way. He specialises in unusual interdisciplinary combinations, thought-provoking narratives and unadulterated silliness!

name: Cloudfactory Design Lisa Gort
description: My background is designing and building inflatable installations door and indoor spaces. I also run creative workshops at festivals and events for young people, children.I co -founded a company called Spacecadets in 2000s and worked with hundreds of city councils, art and music festivals and corporate clients in the UK and Europe.After having kids my career switched to education, and I worked for 15 years as a Lecturer in Art and Design, specialising textiles and 3D. I love working with people of all ages, making art accessible, encouraging people to play, use their imagination.It’s exciting f

name: CoDa Dance Company
description: Our story began in 2013, when our Founder and Artistic Director Nikki Watson discovered a lack of dance for people with Multiple Sclerosis (MS). Nikki had always wanted to choreograph and create exciting experiences, but hadn’t known it would end up being so intrinsically linked to her own life. Her mother had recently been diagnosed with the neurological condition and choreography became a way to process and express Nikki’s situation. At the same time she could see the impact dance was having on people with Parkinson’s and wanted a similar provision for other neurological disabilities like MS

name: Codswallop CIC
description: Codswallop CIC has been creating raucous and lively, family Theatre experiences in traditional and outdoor settings since 2013. Over the last decade, we have built a reputation for chaotic shows with big spectacle moments that leave lasting impressions on audiences of all ages; being big, loud and spectacular is what Codswallop CIC does best. Codswallop CIC also has a wealth of experience in the walkabout Theatre sector, performing by commission at festivals such as Just So, Burnley Canal Festival, So Festival – Magna Vitae, Bradford Literature Festival, Leeds 2023 My Leeds Summer, as well as
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative, Venue

name: Company Fox
description: Company Fox, led by artistic director Hannah Fox, are a wild and wonderful collective of artists, musicians, performers and technicians. We make beautiful, distinctive, playful and sometimes strange events, performances, environments and moments both in but mostly out of doors.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative

name: Company of Others
description: Company of Others is a dance theatre company based in the North East of England, led by Artistic Director and Choreographer Nadia Iftkhar. We collaborate with people who have experience of being ‘othered’ by the society we live in, to co-create dance theatre experiences which highlight and share stories of the human experience through their lens. Our values are Artistry, Diversity and Integrity.

name: Continental Drifts
description: Continental Drifts was started in 1996 by friends with a shared passion for events. Those same friends run the company today from our barge on the river Lea. A unique company with expertise in all aspects of events. Our team of industry specialists work across event management, licensing, health and safety, experiential brand activation, artist booking and programming. With a multiple award-winning production team, we take pride and dedication in offering a 360 production service.
member_type: Agency, Festival, Producer / Programmer / Administrator, Safety / Security

name: Craig West Lighting
description: An experienced lighting designer in Theatre, dance and circus genres. He undertakes several lighting design projects a year and has been working professionally as a lighting designer for 15 years. He has worked with and created work for The National theatre, RSC, LIFT, Emergency exit Arts, Gorilla Circus, Flute Theatre and Appetite Stoke.
region: Lighting Designer Specialist in Outdoor Theatre and Circus Lighting Design

name: Creative Basildon
description: BasildONCreative People and Places is a radical, new cultural programme for people living, working and socialising in Basildon borough.

name: Creative Crawley
description: Creative Crawley is an arts charity working to support, produce and present high quality arts and cultural activity in Crawley, West Sussex. Working in partnership with local, national and international artists, local businesses and other key stakeholders in the town and beyond, we produce a year round programme of creative activities across all artforms that happen in many different spaces and places across the town. Our aim is to develop Crawley as a creative place beyond its boundaries for the benefit of those that live, study, work and play there. Following a public consultation by Crawley
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Festival, Local Authority / BID / CPP, Producer / Programmer / Administrator, Production Manager / Technician / Maker

name: Creative Heights
description: We are a contemporary circus company dedicated to making circus accessible to every body. Through dynamic workshops, outdoor performances, and a blend of aerial and ground-based circus arts, we create inclusive spaces where people of all ages, abilities, and backgrounds can experience the magic of movement and performance. Rooted in the ethos of “Circus for Every BODY,” our work redefines what circus can be—joyful, empowering, and for everyone.
member_type: Educational Organisation, Performance Company, Performer / Artist / Creative

name: Crying Out Loud
description: Crying Out Loud was founded in 2002 by producer-curator Rachel Clare, supported by a Promoters Award from the Combined Arts Department at Arts Council England. Rachel, alongside then co-director Emma Gladstone, had a vision to make a new annual season of international visual performance in London. With a core team and an expanding pool of regularly collaborating freelancers, the company has evolved over the years, but maintained an aesthetic of  memorable, extraordinary and visual  work that crosses art-form boundaries. Since its inception, Crying Out Loud has toured productions, created devel

name: Cscape
description: Cscape began in 2003 with a commitment to making work that was fun, blending imaginative physical theatre with energetic choreography. From beaches to mines, harbours to gardens, rural to midscale theatre venues, we are committed to ensuring our work translates across venue size and location. To do this, we collaborate with likeminded dancers, actors, writers, film makers, designers and musicians. This results in stories that are punchy, absorbing and full of character; perfect for all ages.

name: Curious Company
description: Starting with the Mutoid Waste Company in the 1980s and a decade of stage shows with big techno party production company Megadog, led me to a career of immersive, interactive performance in non-theatre spaces. I trained at Fool Time and set up Curious Company in 1994. The main aim of my practice is to involve the audience as much as possible, for them to have the experience of being inside the story and as it happens to do some of the work! As a result we are sharing the joy of performing the shows and leaving the audience with more than just a vision of our work.

name: Darryl J Carrington
description: Right now I’m a visual comedian, combining everything I’ve learnt in my 2o years experience to create shows that are funny, family friendly and multi disciplinary. I perform non verbal physical comedy shows and specialise in performing at outdoor arts festivals in the UK and across the world.

name: Daryl & Co
description: Daryl & Co is a Disabled-led children’s theatre company where collaboration and co-creation meet mischief and merriment. We make accessible theatre to delight young audiences and their families and we tour all over the country. Our vision is a world where Disabled artists and young people have unrestricted choices to creative experiences and careers. We take fun seriously. Working nationally and internationally, we create colourful and entertaining children’s shows that challenge exclusion and champion equality. We are generous and playful in how we work. To us, the ‘& Co’ is just as important

name: Daughters of the Dust
description: Daughters of the Dust are an all female collective of wire walkers, redefining the traditional narrative surrounding tightwire walking. We create medium to large scale outdoor performances features an all-female cast of high-level, multi-disciplinary artists always with an emphasis on wire walking. Shows –ElevateHer is an all female wire walking show, played out across three multi level interconnecting wires, telling the stories of six performers through music, spoken word and movement. With an emphasis on female camaraderie and empowerment.

name: Deb Mullins
description: Deb Mullins has an extensive and distinguished career in outdoor and participatory arts. Her career spans five decades and as one of the founder members (1980) – now member for life – and Artistic Director ofEmergency Exit Arts;she was responsible for building the organisation’s outstanding reputation for unforgettable outdoor performances, parades and spectacular events across the UK and Europe. As a pioneer of outdoor arts for over 30 years, Deb has extensive experience of working with a broad range of artists using most art forms and she has directed and produced:  carnivals and lantern pro
member_type: Performer / Artist / Creative, Producer / Programmer / Administrator

name: Displace Yourself Theatre
description: Displace Yourself Theatre work in collaboration with people who have experienced Displacement. This may mean someone displaced from their country, their family home, their friendship group or even from their body and emotions. On some level, perhaps we can all identify with being forcibly removed from something we belong to. It is this link, and this element of the human experience, which interests DYT and forms the basis of all our conversations, workshops, training, productions and creative sessions.

name: Dizzy O’Dare
city: delighting audiences across the globe. The company aims to promote art and culture to areas with little access or engagement to great art while presenting work of the highest standard.
description: Dizzy O’Dare (DOD) is a contemporary circus & theatre company established in 2009 by Alana Jones and Michael Imerson. They have created a diverse range of work, which has toured extensively, delighting audiences across the globe. The company aims to promote art and culture to areas with little access or engagement to great art while presenting work of the highest standard. Alana Jones(UK) is a dancer and tightwire artist graduating from Laban, London and National Centre for Circus Arts, London. Alana performs across the world and also manages events, cabarets and festivals working with a varie
region: Dizzy O’Dare (DOD) is a contemporary circus & theatre company established in 2009 by Alana Jones and Michael Imerson. They have created a diverse range of work, which has toured extensively, delighting audiences across the globe. The company aims to promote art and culture to areas with little access or engagement to great art while presenting work of the highest standard.

name: Dock Street Events
description: We work collaboratively with our clients, from initial consultancy to final delivery, bringing together creatives, collaborators and suppliers with the right skills and experience to deliver high-quality memorable events. We work with a range of clients in the public and private sectors, including local authorities, creative producers, festivals, charities, one-off events and theatres. Our work includes outdoor arts, public spectacles, sports ceremonies, theatre, opera and charity events.
member_type: Agency, Production Manager / Technician / Maker, Safety / Security, Support Organisation

name: DoodleJam / Scott Walker / Up Against The Wall
description: Scott Walkeris a muralist, illustrator and painter who specialises in public event works and interactive mural installations. Utilising a background in graphic design and illustration Scott honed a career in making visually arresting art on a scale that captures imagination.
region: Scott Walker creates irresistible participatory art before your eyes.

name: Dream Engine
description: We create unique and unusual performance installations. A show where the structure, performance, soundtrack and location create a sum larger than the parts. The [Read More >](https://outdoorartsuk.org/members-listing/the-dream-engine/)

name: Driveabout Projection
description: DRIVEABOUT Projection is a crowd-stopping, ex-military customised vehicle, which is self-powered and infinitely flexible. This vintage VW LT35 was recently imported from mainland Europe & it’s most popular uses over the last 10 years include Projection-Mapping, Mural Canvas, Guerrilla Marketing & Outdoor Cinema & Performance…separately or all at same time. Originally a mobile radar tower, this makes it perfect for Video Projection outdoors, also a secure base for Lasers & Lighting, ideal for health & safety requirements. Currently submitting to Light Festivals as a park-up interactive Projecti

name: Dulce Duca
description: Dulce Duca conceives absurd and surrealistic shows, that will make you laugh or run away. She never won any prize but she thinks the audience loves her.

name: Earth-bound
description: Earth-bound is the work of performer, theatre & film maker Sioda Adams. Working with a collaborative approach through a mix of art forms she presents high quality, authentic performance art that is bold, brave and emotional. She aims to provoke thoughts and feelings that impact social and environmental issues.

name: EggPeople
description: EggPeople are an innovative and dynamic performing arts organization that specialises in captivating audiences through immersive and interactive street theatre experiences. With an anarchic and playful style, EggPeople bring stories to life in unexpected outdoor settings, delighting audiences of all ages. We like to explore space, boundaries, environment and people’s perspectives, and aspire to create work that is inclusive and accessible and socially relevant. Egg People make high quality, entertaining, visually striking work. Drawing upon their skills in clown, improvisation and ensemble wor

name: Eilidh Bryan
description: Creating art is my way of trying to make the world a little bit better and giving people something to smile about. I believe in people, and enjoy making work that creates connection – whether it is through a community project, interactive puppetry performance, or a shared theatrical experience. ​I have been lucky enough to work with some amazing artists over the years, and have produced work for some incredible companies. In particular, I have a close relationship with the Lantern Company, with whom I have been a core artist for 20 years and have made many memorable puppets, lanterns and parad

name: Electric Medway
description: We are an arts organisation working with communities and new technology across Medway. Our flagship Electric Medway Festival is open to everyone, providing a unique platform for people of all backgrounds to pioneer new art.
member_type: Creation Centre / Commissioning Organisation, Educational Organisation, Festival, Producer / Programmer / Administrator

name: Ellen Booth
description: I’m a motivated, caring and value-led freelance Creative Arts Producer & Project / Programme Manager, with 10 years of experience gained within National Portfolio Organisations, International Arts Festivals and direct collaborations with artists & companies. Working across art forms and locations – outdoor, indoor and site specific. With a deep interest in delivering projects that are culturally democratic with focus on supporting disability arts, co-creation & community engagement, I love working in unexpected settings in interesting ways.
region: England, Midlands, England, South East, England, South West

name: Elyssa Fagan
description: I’m a creative marketing consultant with over 14 years of experience offering communications services to the Arts and Culture sector. I went freelance in 2019 after years of leading teams within creative agencies across the UK. During my career I have been in charge of creating effective cross-channel marketing and PR strategies for a wide variety of clients. From working with small start-ups and SMEs, right through to large organisations like LEGO, SockShop and Grosvenor Casinos as well as the artist Paul Cummins MBE, my experience is vast. My focus has shifted over the years to specialise in

name: Emergency Exit Arts
description: Emergency Exit Arts (EEA) is one of Britain’s pioneering outdoor arts companies, creating magical experiences in the places where you live, learn, work and play. We reinvigorate communities, give people a voice and challenge the ordinary. Founded in 1980, EEA began as an artists’ collective born out of a desire to radically transform the ordinary into the extraordinary. At a time of social and political change, we aspired to make a positive difference to people’s lives, particularly those who were discriminated against and those living in economically excluded communities across London and the

name: Enter Edem
description: Creating a world of playful perspectives, we tell bonkers and brilliant engaging stories to family audiences. We are lively, astonishing and interactive, appearing and surprising on streets, theatres and unlikely locations, bringing life to a plethora of spaces. Our productions and acts entice anyone coming into our world to be engaged, engrossed and enthralled in the experience from the moment they Enter Edem.

name: Essex Cultural Diversity Project
description: Essex Cultural Diversity Project energises cultural diversity in arts and heritage. Our mission is to create opportunities, stimulate participation and provide a focal point for the development, celebration and co-ordination of cultural diversity through arts and heritage activity. We achieve this through an exciting programme of projects, festivals and events; commissioning artists to explore community, diversity and place; and supporting artists, arts organisations and communities working in diversity through training and networking events, signposting opportunities and providing important p

name: Etta Ermini Dance Theatre
description: Etta Erminitrained in drama and dance in Belgium and also completed a MA in European Dance Theatre Practice at Laban. Etta Ermini Dance Theatre was founded in 2005 with the aim to explore relationships through cross art form and storytelling techniques. The physical theatre company works with an interdisciplinary approach examining relationships through dance, theatre, spoken word and other creative disciplines. In extracting elements of the absurd, the company uncovers the dark humour in dramatic events and together with audience interaction weaves this into a series of theatrical vignettes f

name: Feasible Ferret Theatre
description: Feasible Ferret Theatre creates playful, eye catching and interactive work for outdoor events and community settings. Our aim is simple: to spark joy and bring people together through laughter. We take our acts to events and communities across the UK, working successfully with rural touring schemes and bringing performances to community events, rural areas and places with limited access to arts engagement. In addition to rural touring, we perform at larger arts festivals and events, including SIRF, SO Festival and Arts Fresco. Feasible Ferret Theatre has over a decade of experience creating en

name: Fee Hudson Francis
description: I am a freelance producer currently working as Project Manager for Hat Fair – the UK’s longest running street arts festival and assistant producer and access coordinator for Galatea – a outdoor theatre show with Emma Frankland, Wildworks and Marlborough Productions. My focus is on access led work and I am on a personal development journey to become a more inclusive Outdoor Arts producer. My previous work includes mass participation sports events management, corporate venue management and community-based arts projects. My degree was in Drama and Event Management and my dissertation focused on t

name: Feet off the Ground
description: Feet off the Ground creates transformative, relatable outdoor dance productions which comment on socio- political issues through a feminist lens. The work reflects the company’s collective belief in the therapeutic benefit of movement, human connection and touch, and their democratic and collaborative approach to creating dance.​ The core collective is made up of 4 artists: Lucia, Patricia, Robyn and Sophie who work in a democratic, non-hierarchical way both in and out of the studio. Feet off the Ground are proud to present an ensemble solely managed and performed by women.
region: England, London, England, Midlands, England, North, England, South East, England, South West, Europe, Ireland, Northern Ireland, Other, Scotland, Wales

name: Feral
description: Feralare a Glasgow based arts producing house founded in 2015 byJill SmithandKathryn Boyle. Together we produce a portfolio of artists, tour work locally and internationally, organise festivals and develop creative residencies as a way of supporting a diverse community of independent artists working across the visual theatre and outdoor arts sector in Scotland. As a producing house we act as vital support mechanism for Scotland based artists to nourish their practice, realise the ambitions of their work and elevate their profile. We prioritise working with artists who expand notions of what co

name: Festival of Fools
description: The festival is the largest comedy, circus and outdoor arts festival on the island of Ireland, with over 70 performances taking place on the streets of Belfast City Centre and Cathedral Quarter. All daytime shows are free to attend and apart from the shows in our Circusful building, no booking is required.

name: Festival of Thrift
description: Festival of Thrift builds a creative community through sharing skills and ideas for our sustainable future. We have been celebrating sustainability since 2012.  We promote the concept of sustainable living and are a voice for sustainable issues globally and locally.

name: Fettle & Fable
description: Lisa Difford – Artistic Director Lisa is an award-winning theatre maker, actor and storyteller with a passion for enabling people to explore the world around them in engaging, accessible & meaningful ways. An established performer in regional theatres and at the Olivier Stage at The National, Lisa has devised performances for the National Coal Mining Museum as well for Barnsley Museums where her work contributed to their status as finalist in the 2021 Museum of the Year award. Lisa’s story films, created during COVID-19 lockdown, won the Family Friendly Museum Award for Best Film 2020 and were

name: Folk Dance Remixed
description: Folk Dance Remixed is a fresh, quirky, colourful and magical collision of traditional, Hip Hop and Folk dance and music styles: high-energy, with a unique remix of Maypole, Clogging, Ceilidh, Street, House and Breakdance with a hint of African & Bollywood… to a live soundtrack of fiddle & beat boxing – this is Folk and Street Dance like you’ve never seen it before! Led by Artistic Directors Kerry Fletcher and Natasha Khamjani, we rediscover old traditions through contemporary forms, and take it out on the streets through performances, Street Dance the Maypole workshops and participatory Ceilid

name: Fontanella Dance
description: As a choreographer/dance artistKristyn Fontanellafocuses her past knowledge of Irish step dancing and presents it in a contemporary context. Having toured for many years with Riverdance, Lord of the Dance & as lead soloist in Gaelforce Dance, her current mission is to show another side of the complex world of Irish Step dancing to future generations. Kristyn’s duet with Sibéal Davitt, ‘As We Know It’ was an early success, showing in NYC, Dublin Dance Festival, Marseille New Danse Festival, & Tanzmesse Germany. Her ensemble work, IN LiMBO, has had continued support from the Arts Council, and ha

name: Fools Paradise
description: Fool’s Paradiseis an international street performance agency founded in 1991. We supply the best UK street theatre and outdoor arts to regional and worldwide audiences. We champion the most inspiring, wondrous and downright ridiculous talent. Our networks and friendships span over twenty years. With artist coverage across the UK we can programme performers in your area or make travel arrangements for your must-have international acts. We love to place artists in unexpected spaces and have performed in city streets, shopping centres, festival fields, at parties and illuminated parades.

description: Gorilla Circus produces large scale outdoor circus shows, assists TV & Film companies add circus acts or effects to their productions and provides entertainment [Read More >](https://outdoorartsuk.org/members-listing/gorilla-circus/)

name: Great British Carnival
description: We create vibrant and exciting parades and events. We showcase the excellence and diversity of all the global Carnival styles in the UK. We [Read More >](https://outdoorartsuk.org/members-listing/gb-carnival/)

name: Just More Productions
description: Just More productions are a community engagement company, using food history and performance to educate and entertain, we produce vibrant and friendly educational performances bringing cultures and communities together. “Do What Yah Mother Told Yah!” our most recent production is a feast of food fusion and storytelling across geography and generations, in this immersive and vibrant comedy cooking show.

name: Mr Holly
description: I am a freelance creative producer working across Lincolnshire and the Midlands and in a former life was Director of Lincoln Drill Hall for [Read More >](https://outdoorartsuk.org/members-listing/mr-holly/)

name: Oceanallover
description: Oceanallover is a site-specific theatre arts company representing the work of Artistic Director Alex Rigg. Alex Rigg has been making live events since 1982. These have taken a number of forms and have been shown in several different countries. If there is a common thread to the style and content of these events then it must be a strong need to push boundaries around, to set foot on unknown soil and generally to play with our concepts of art outside its accepted form. He trained as an archaeological illustrator, drystone dyker, blacksmith, fine artist, dancer, timber framer and costume maker/de
